205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass
304

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3
1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'
1031	    print(print_info)
1032	
1033	    # Check first_conversion format
1034	    if   first_conversion == "f16"  : pass
1035	    elif first_conversion == "bf16" : pass
1036	    elif first_conversion == "f32"  : pass
1037	    elif first_conversion == "q8_0" : pass
1038	    else:
1039	        raise RuntimeError(
1040	            f"Unsloth: `first_conversion` can only be one of ['f16', 'bf16', 'f32', 'q8_0'] and not `{first_conversion}`."
1041	        )
1042	    pass
1043	
1044	    # Determine whether the system already has llama.cpp installed and the scripts are executable
1045	    quantize_location = get_executable(["llama-quantize", "quantize", "llama-quantize.exe", "quantize.exe"])
1046	    convert_location  = get_executable(["convert-hf-to-gguf.py", "convert_hf_to_gguf.py"])
1047	
1048	    error = 0
1049	    if quantize_location is not None and convert_location is not None:
1050	        print("Unsloth: llama.cpp found in the system. We shall skip installation.")
1051	    else:
1052	        print("Unsloth: Installing llama.cpp. This might take 3 minutes...")
1053	        if _run_installer is not None:
1054	            _run_installer, IS_CMAKE = _run_installer
1055	
1056	            error = _run_installer.wait()
1057	            # Check if successful
1058	            if error != 0:
1059	                print(f"Unsloth: llama.cpp error code = {error}.")
1060	                install_llama_cpp_old(-10)
1061	            pass
1062	
1063	            if IS_CMAKE:
1064	                # CMAKE needs to do some extra steps
1065	                print("Unsloth: CMAKE detected. Finalizing some steps for installation.")
1066	
1067	                check = os.system("cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp")
1068	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1069	                check = os.system("rm -rf llama.cpp/build")
1070	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1071	            pass
1072	        else:
1073	            error = 0
1074	            install_llama_cpp_blocking()
1075	        pass
1076	
1077	        # Careful llama.cpp/quantize changed to llama.cpp/llama-quantize
1078	        # and llama.cpp/main changed to llama.cpp/llama-cli
1079	        # See https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/pull/7809
1080	        quantize_location = None
1081	        if os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize.exe"):
1082	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize.exe"
1083	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize"):
1084	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize"
1085	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"):
1086	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"
1087	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize"):
1088	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize"
1089	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"):
1090	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"
1091	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"):
1092	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"
1093	        else:
1094	            raise RuntimeError(
1095	                "Unsloth: The file 'llama.cpp/llama-quantize' or `llama.cpp/quantize` does not exist.\n"\
1096	                "We've also double checked the building directory under 'llama.cpp/build/bin/'.\n"\
1097	                "But we expect this file to exist! Check if the file exists under llama.cpp and investigate the building process of llama.cpp (make/cmake)!"
1098	            )
1099	        pass

1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",

1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",

1827	    del arguments["self"]
1828	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
1829	    del arguments["first_conversion"]

1833	
1834	    # Non blocking install GGUF first
1835	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
1836	
1837	        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
1838	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
1839	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1840	            python_install.wait()
1841	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
1842	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1843	            makefile = None
1844	        else:
1845	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
1846	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1847	            git_clone.wait()
1848	            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
1849	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1850	            python_install.wait()
1851	        pass
1852	    else:
1853	        try:
1854	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1855	            makefile = None
1856	        except:
1857	            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp
1858	            if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
1859	                # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
1860	                python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1861	                python_install.wait()
1862	                install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
1863	                new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1864	                makefile = None
1865	            else:

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors

## Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase. Here's a comprehensive analysis of its structure and key components:

### 1. Overall Directory Structure

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main entry point and package initialization
├── _auto_install.py            # Auto-install utilities
├── save.py                      # Save/export functionality (2678 lines - MAIN FILE)
├── tokenizer_utils.py           # Tokenizer utilities
├── trainer.py                   # Training utilities
├── chat_templates.py            # Chat template management
├── import_fixes.py              # Import compatibility fixes
├── kernels/                     # Custom CUDA/Triton kernels
│   ├── fast_lora.py             # LoRA forward/backward kernels
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py    # Loss function kernels
│   ├── rms_layernorm.py         # Layer norm kernels
│   ├── rope_embedding.py        # Rotary embedding kernels

│   ├── utils.py                 # Kernel utilities (get_lora_parameters, fast_dequantize, etc.)
│   └── moe/                     # Mixture of Experts kernels
├── models/                      # Model architecture implementations
│   ├── llama.py                 # LLaMA/Mistral base class (FastLlamaModel)
│   ├── loader.py                # Main loader (FastLanguageModel, FastModel, FastVisionModel)
│   ├── vision.py                # Vision models base class (FastBaseModel)
│   ├── mistral.py               # Mistral-specific optimizations
│   ├── qwen2.py, qwen3.py       # Qwen models
│   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py      # Gemma models
│   ├── granite.py               # Granite models
│   ├── cohere.py                # Cohere models
│   ├── falcon_h1.py             # Falcon H1 models
│   ├── _utils.py                # Utility functions for models
│   ├── mapper.py                # Model mapping utilities
│   ├── dpo.py                   # DPO trainer patches
│   └── rl.py                    # RL trainer patches
├── registry/                    # Model registry
└── utils/                       # Utility modules
    └── hf_hub.py                # Hugging Face Hub utilities
```

---

### 2. Save and Export Methods (Primary File: `/unsloth/save.py`)

**Core Save/Export Functions:**

| Function | Location | Signature | Purpose |
|----------|----------|-----------|---------|
| `unsloth_save_model()` | Line 205 | `(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method="lora", push_to_hub=False, ...)` | Main unified save function supporting three save methods |
| `save_to_gguf()` | Line 952 | `(model_type, model_dtype, is_sentencepiece=False, model_directory="unsloth_finetuned_model", quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)` | Converts models to GGUF format via llama.cpp |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` | Line 1768 | `(self, save_directory, tokenizer=None, quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)` | Model method: Save as GGUF with auto-conversion from 4bit to float16 |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` | Line 1321 | `(self, save_directory, tokenizer=None, ...)` | Model method: Merge LoRA and save (16-bit or 4-bit) |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` | Line 1364 | `(self, repo_id, tokenizer=None, ...)` | Model method: Merge and push to Hub |
| `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()` | Line 1951 | `(self, repo_id, tokenizer=None, quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)` | Model method: Convert to GGUF and push to Hub |
| `save_lora_to_custom_dir()` | Line 2129 | `(model, tokenizer, save_directory)` | Save only LoRA adapters in custom directory |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` | Line 2143 | `(self, tokenizer, repo_id, ...)` | Convert LoRA adapters to GGML and push to Hub |
| `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` | Line 2211 | `(self, save_directory, tokenizer, ...)` | Convert LoRA to GGML locally |
| `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` | Line 2502 | `(self, save_directory, tokenizer=None, ...)` | Save with TorchAO quantization |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | Line 2569 | `(model, vision=False)` | Patches model with custom save/push methods |

**Save Methods Supported:**
- `"lora"` - Save only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
- `"merged_16bit"` - Merge LoRA into float16 weights (needed for GGUF/llama.cpp)
- `"merged_4bit"` - Merge LoRA into 4bit weights (for inference/DPO)

---

### 3. LoRA Adapter Handling

**LoRA Implementation Files:**

| File | Key Components | Details |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | Fused LoRA forward/backward kernels | Implements `LoRA_MLP`, `LoRA_Attention` custom autograd functions |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | `get_lora_parameters()` | Extracts LoRA matrices from linear layers |
| | `get_lora_parameters_bias()` | Handles bias parameters |
| | `matmul_lora()` | Fused matrix multiplication with LoRA |
| `/unsloth/models/vision.py` | `get_peft_model()` (line 803) | Creates LoRA config and applies PEFT |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | `get_peft_model()` (line 2266) | Base implementation with full parameter control |
| `/unsloth/save.py` | LoRA extraction/merging | Handles adapter loading and merging into base weights |

**LoRA Configuration Parameters (from `get_peft_model`):**
```python
r                          = 16              # LoRA rank
target_modules             = [...]           # Which layers to apply LoRA
lora_alpha                 = 16              # Scaling factor
lora_dropout               = 0.0             # Dropout rate
bias                       = "none"          # Bias handling
use_rslora                 = False           # Rank-Stabilized LoRA
modules_to_save            = None            # Additional modules to save
init_lora_weights          = True            # Initialize weights
loftq_config               = {}              # LoftQ configuration
```

**Key LoRA Operations:**
- LoRA adapters are stored in PEFT format (standard HuggingFace format)
- Merging combines: `W_merged = W_base + (lora_B @ lora_A) * (lora_alpha / r)`
- Supports multiple LoRA adapters (via `adapter_names`)
- Custom kernels provide 2x speedup for LoRA forward/backward passes

---

### 4. ONNX-Related Code

**Finding:** There is **NO existing ONNX support** in the codebase.

Searched for: `onnx`, `ONNX`, `ort_session` - **No matches found**.

The codebase focuses on:
- PyTorch native formats
- GGUF (llama.cpp) export
- HuggingFace safetensors format
- GGML format (for Ollama)

---

### 5. Main Model Classes and Structure

**Class Hierarchy:**

```
FastBaseModel (vision.py)
├── FastModel (loader.py)
│   ├── FastLanguageModel (loader.py - inherits from FastLlamaModel)
│   ├── FastVisionModel (loader.py)
│   └── FastTextModel (loader.py)
└── FastLlamaModel (llama.py) - Base for all text models
    ├── FastMistralModel (mistral.py)
    ├── FastQwen2Model (qwen2.py)
    ├── FastQwen3Model (qwen3.py)
    ├── FastQwen3MoeModel (qwen3_moe.py)
    ├── FastGemmaModel (gemma.py)
    ├── FastGemma2Model (gemma2.py)
    ├── FastGraniteModel (granite.py)
    ├── FastCohereModel (cohere.py)
    └── FastFalconH1Model (falcon_h1.py)
```

**Key Methods in Model Classes:**

| Class | Method | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| FastBaseModel | `from_pretrained()` | Load model with optional 4-bit/8-bit quantization |
| | `get_peft_model()` | Apply LoRA training config |
| | `for_training()` | Switch to training mode |
| | `for_inference()` | Switch to inference mode |
| FastLanguageModel | `from_pretrained()` | Specialized for language models |
| | `get_peft_model()` | Pre-configured defaults for LLMs |
| All Models (via patch) | `save_pretrained()` | Standard HF method (original) |
| | `save_pretrained_merged()` | Unsloth: Merge & save (16-bit or 4-bit) |
| | `save_pretrained_gguf()` | Unsloth: Convert to GGUF format |
| | `save_pretrained_ggml()` | Unsloth: Convert to GGML format |
| | `save_pretrained_torchao()` | Unsloth: Save with TorchAO quantization |
| | `push_to_hub()` | Standard HF method (enhanced by Unsloth) |
| | `push_to_hub_merged()` | Unsloth: Merge & push to Hub |
| | `push_to_hub_gguf()` | Unsloth: Convert to GGUF & push |
| | `push_to_hub_ggml()` | Unsloth: Convert to GGML & push |

---

### 6. Key Code Locations for Reference

**LoRA Parameter Extraction:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Lines 475-532 (forward pass with LoRA)
- `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` - `get_lora_parameters()` function

**Model Patching (where methods are added):**
- `/unsloth/save.py` - Lines 2569-2677 (patch_saving_functions)
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` - Lines 2200+ (post_patch method)

**Standard Save Method Call Chain:**
1. User calls `model.save_pretrained_merged(save_dir, tokenizer)`
2. Routes to `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (added via patching)
3. Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
4. Merges LoRA weights into base model
5. Uses HuggingFace transformers `save_pretrained()` internally

**Test Examples:**
- `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` - Comprehensive save/load tests
- `/tests/saving/language_models/` - Model-specific save tests
- Shows usage patterns for merged saves, GGUF, and Hub uploads

---

### 7. Export Pipeline Examples

**GGUF Export Flow:**
```
model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_dir, tokenizer, quantization_method="q4_k_m")
    ↓
unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf() [line 1768]
    ↓
1. Installs llama.cpp (git clone, make)
    ↓
2. unsloth_save_model() with save_method="merged_16bit"
    ↓
3. save_to_gguf() [line 952]
    ↓
4. Converts HF model to GGUF with convert-hf-to-gguf.py
    ↓
5. Quantizes with llama-quantize tool
    ↓
Returns: GGUF file(s) in specified directory
```

**LoRA-Only Export:**
```
model.save_pretrained(save_dir)
    ↓
unsloth_save_model() with save_method="lora"
    ↓
Saves only LoRA adapter files (adapter_model.bin, adapter_config.json)
    ↓
Can be loaded later with merge via unsloth or standard PEFT
```

---

### 8. Important Configuration Constants

**From `/unsloth/save.py`:**
- `LLAMA_WEIGHTS` - Default layers to apply LoRA (Q, K, V, O, gate, up, down projections)
- `LLAMA_LAYERNORMS` - Layer norm modules for optional LoRA
- `ALLOWED_QUANTS` - Dict of supported GGUF quantization methods (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.)

**From `/unsloth/__init__.py`:**
- `DEVICE_TYPE` - "cuda", "hip", or "xpu"
- `DEVICE_COUNT` - Number of available devices
- `SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16` - BF16 capability check

---

### 9. Integration Points for ONNX Export

If implementing ONNX export, you would need to:

1. **Add conversion function** → Similar structure to `save_to_gguf()` at line 952 in save.py
2. **Hook into patch_saving_functions()** → Add method like `save_pretrained_onnx` (line 2664-2667)
3. **Handle LoRA merging first** → Use existing `unsloth_save_model()` with merged weights
4. **Consider quantization options** → Could follow GGUF pattern (INT8, QInt8, etc.)
5. **Leverage existing kernels** → LoRA merging already optimized, just needs ONNX export wrapper

**Key Dependencies:**
- PEFT library for LoRA handling (already imported)
- PyTorch with ONNX export support (torch.onnx.export)
- Optional: ONNXRuntime for validation

---

This completes the comprehensive exploration of the unsloth codebase structure!

# limitations under the License.

import triton
import ctypes
MAX_FUSED_SIZE : int = 65536
next_power_of_2 = triton.next_power_of_2
import functools
from typing import Optional
from unsloth import DEVICE_TYPE, DEVICE_COUNT

# torch.cuda.amp.custom_fwd is deprecated >= 2.4
import torch
torch_Tensor = torch.Tensor
from packaging.version import Version

if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and Version(torch.__version__) < Version("2.6.0"):
    raise RuntimeError("Intel xpu currently supports unsloth with torch.version >= 2.6.0")

if Version(torch.__version__) < Version("2.4.0"):
    torch_amp_custom_fwd = torch.cuda.amp.custom_fwd
    torch_amp_custom_bwd = torch.cuda.amp.custom_bwd
else:
    torch_amp_custom_fwd = torch.amp.custom_fwd(device_type = "cuda")
    torch_amp_custom_bwd = torch.amp.custom_bwd(device_type = "cuda")
pass

if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    torch_amp_custom_fwd = torch.amp.custom_fwd(device_type = "xpu")
    torch_amp_custom_bwd = torch.amp.custom_bwd(device_type = "xpu")


# tl.math.tanh now is libdevice.tanh
from packaging.version import Version
import triton
import triton.language as tl
if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
    if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        triton_tanh = tl.extra.intel.libdevice.tanh
    else:
        from triton.language.extra import libdevice
        triton_tanh = libdevice.tanh
    triton_cast = tl.cast
else:
    triton_tanh = tl.math.tanh
    # No casting in old Triton versions
    @triton.jit
    def triton_cast(x, dtype):
        return x.to(dtype)
    pass
pass


def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
    num_warps : int = 4
    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps
pass

HAS_CUDA_STREAM = False
# INTEL GPU specific logic
if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # TODO: Changed here after adding XPU BNB support

from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
import subprocess
import psutil
import re
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi
try:
    from huggingface_hub import get_token
except:
    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token
    except:
        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass
from pathlib import Path

__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())

185	torch_float16  = torch.float16
186	torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

188	def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)
189	
190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass
233	
234	
235	def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
236	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
237	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
246	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
247	    adapter = adapter[0]
248	
249	    return (
250	        W,
251	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
254	        proj.scaling[adapter],
255	        base_layer.bias,
256	    )
257	pass
258	
259	
260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass

def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")

def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass

)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
    "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
